forbidden_patterns.description: A domain cannot import another domain repository or persistence package.

Database clients are limited to approved persistence packages, except that registered composition-root paths (`requirements.composition_roots`) may import a database client solely to construct the single process pool/connection and inject it through published ports, per ADR-0027. No other path may import a database client.
Provider SDK types cannot appear in domain contracts.
UI packages cannot own authoritative business rules or persistence.
Commercial plan names cannot appear in authorization logic.
Feature flags cannot create entitlement access.
Business domains cannot import Better Auth tables or persistence models.
AI prompt and tool packages cannot import repositories directly.
Domain packages cannot import self-hosted or cloud-specific infrastructure code.
Runtime-neutral core packages cannot import Bun globals or Bun-only APIs.
Hono request and context objects remain in application transport adapters.
oRPC transport objects remain in application transport adapters or generated-client boundaries.
Contracts and authorization policies cannot import database adapters.
An owner-specific persistence package cannot import another owner's repository, private schema, tables, or migrations.
Pool creation, shutdown, and connection-bearing environment reads belong to registered composition roots.

exceptions.violates: ADR-0027 owner-specific persistence and composition-root connection lifecycle
exceptions.description: The pre-WS1 scaffold keeps Better Auth's Drizzle schema, migration, pool, and environment access inside platform/identity. PR1 makes the architecture rule executable without pretending this known debt is already removed; WS1 PR2 relocates these artifacts into owner-specific Persistence and composition packages before adding another schema owner.
exceptions.expires_when: WS1 PR2 merges the ADR-0027 owner-specific Platform Identity Persistence adapter and composition-owned pool
exceptions.recheck_trigger: Every WS1 PR; this exception blocks PR2 merge and any new schema-owning package
exceptions.risk: A runtime-neutral Platform package still owns concrete database and process-resource lifecycle concerns until PR2.
exceptions.compensating_control: Path-aware CI limits the exception to the named package and rules; no new schema owner may use it.
exceptions.removal_plan: Move schema/migrations to packages/persistence/platform-identity-postgres and pool lifecycle to apps/server/composition in WS1 PR2.
exceptions.approval: PDA-RDM-008 §3 G3 and ADR-0027 (Proposed controlled-prototype guidance; specialist review pending)
